analysis.text:
This image is a choropleth map of Karnataka districts in India, depicting the Gross District Domestic Product (GDDP) in crores of rupees. The map uses a color gradient to represent the economic output of each district, with darker shades indicating higher GDDP values and lighter shades indicating lower GDDP values.

The color scale on the right side of the map indicates the GDDP ranges:
- Light yellow represents districts with GDDP of 20,000 or less.
- Light orange represents districts with GDDP between 30,000 and 40,000.
- Orange represents districts with GDDP between 50,000 and 60,000.
- Red represents districts with GDDP between 70,000 and 80,000.
- Dark red represents districts with GDDP between 90,000 and 100,000.
- Darkest red represents districts with GDDP of 100,000 or more.

The map includes the names of all 30 districts in Karnataka, with the most economically prosperous districts, such as Bengaluru Urban and Bengaluru Rural, shown in the darkest red, indicating the highest GDDP values. The least economically prosperous districts are shown in the lightest yellow.

The source of the data is the Department of Economics & Statistics, Government of Karnataka (2023-24).
